Fidelity Investments: Overview & Ratings

Fidelity Investments is a leading US broker, consistently rated highly for its research tools, zero-fee index funds, and customer service. As of March 2026, Fidelity scores 4.7/5 on TradingToolsHub. Fidelity is best for long-term investors, retirement planning, and those who value in-depth research. For the latest details, visit the official Fidelity website.

Fidelity Investments Pricing: 2026 Breakdown

Fidelity's core pricing is simple:

  • Account Minimum: $0
  • Stock & ETF Trades: $0 commission
  • Zero-Fee Index Funds: $0 expense ratio

Fidelity offers free trading for US stocks and ETFs, and its proprietary index funds have no management fees. For other products or advanced services, pricing is not verified in the supplied data. For the most current and detailed fee schedule, refer to the official source.

Hidden Fees & Cost Transparency

According to TradingToolsHub's latest data, Fidelity is transparent about its core pricing. There are no hidden fees for basic stock and ETF trades or for its zero-fee index funds. However, if you are considering options, mutual funds outside Fidelity's zero-fee lineup, or other advanced services, specific fees are not verified here. Always review the official fee disclosures before opening an account.

Key Features & Who Fidelity Is Best For

  • Best-in-class research and analysis tools
  • Zero-fee index funds with no expense ratio
  • Excellent customer service, consistently rated #1

Fidelity is ideal for:

  • Long-term investors
  • Retirement planning
  • Research-driven investors

Cons: Fidelity's Active Trader Pro platform is not as powerful as ThinkorSwim, and there is no cryptocurrency trading available.

For a full breakdown, see our Fidelity Investments review.

For more on Fidelity's strengths and weaknesses, read our Fidelity Investments review.

TradingToolsHub Recommendation

Fidelity Investments stands out in 2026 for its $0 commissions, zero-fee index funds, and top-rated customer service. If you are a long-term or retirement-focused investor who values research and cost transparency, Fidelity is a leading choice. However, if you need advanced trading tools or cryptocurrency access, consider alternatives. For any unverified pricing or features, consult the official Fidelity website before making your decision.
